channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Appeals court rejects HUD homelessness overhaul saying it would be "disastrous"
item.description: The federal housing agency wants to shift money away from permanent housing and toward programs that impose sobriety and other conditions. Advocates warn that would push many back into homelessness.
item.content:encoded:
The federal housing agency wants to shift money away from permanent housing and toward programs that impose sobriety and other conditions. Advocates warn that would push many back into homelessness.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: FIFA hikes World Cup prices, again. A finals ticket can now cost as much as $11,000
item.description: FIFA has raised prices for over 40 games of the World Cup in its latest sale that kicked off Wednesday, according to an NPR count. The most expensive tickets to the final now cost $10,900.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA has raised prices for over 40 games of the World Cup in its latest sale that kicked off Wednesday, according to an NPR count. The most expensive tickets to the final now cost $10,900.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: Republicans in Congress say they have a deal to end the record-long shutdown at DHS
item.description: The plan would fund DHS, except for immigration enforcement, through September. Republicans would then try to fund the whole agency for three years using a tactic that would not need Democratic votes.
item.content:encoded:
The plan would fund DHS, except for immigration enforcement, through September. Republicans would then try to fund the whole agency for three years using a tactic that would not need Democratic votes.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: The FDA approves a new obesity pill, giving patients another option
item.description: The daily pill called Foundayo got a fast track through the Food and Drug Administration. It will compete with the pill form of Wegovy as an alternative to obesity drugs given by injection.
item.content:encoded: The daily pill called Foundayo got a fast track through the Food and Drug Administration. It will compete with the pill form of Wegovy as an alternative to obesity drugs given by injection.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
item.description: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.
item.content:encoded:
Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

(Image credit: Suy Se)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: "Lives will be lost": How the U.K.'s aid cuts may affect parts of Africa
item.description: In March, the UK announced it would trim its global aid budget and set new priorities in 2027. This has some countries and organizations worried that on top of the US aid cuts, this could be unsurvivable.
item.content:encoded:
In March, the UK announced it would trim its global aid budget and set new priorities in 2027. This has some countries and organizations worried that on top of the US aid cuts, this could be unsurvivable.

(Image credit: Luis Tato/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: This fringe actor finds the spotlight in Israel's most provocative film post-Oct. 7
item.description: Israeli fringe actor in the spotlight for anti-war film
item.content:encoded:
It landed him a best actor nomination at Israel's academy award ceremony last year. He didn't win.'/>

Israeli fringe actor in the spotlight for anti-war film

(Image credit: Ofir Berman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Trump makes his case for war with Iran, saying the conflict is 'nearing completion'
item.description: Trump used a prime-time address to outline his objectives for the war and discuss the military's progress to date. He also repeated an earlier claim that fighting could end in two to three weeks.
item.content:encoded:
Trump used a prime-time address to outline his objectives for the war and discuss the military's progress to date. He also repeated an earlier claim that fighting could end in two to three weeks.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Supreme Court majority seems inclined to rule against Trump on birthright citizenship
item.description: The majority seemed skeptical of the Trump administration's argument on birthright citizenship, appearing ready to rule in favor of upholding automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil.
item.content:encoded:
The majority seemed skeptical of the Trump administration's argument on birthright citizenship, appearing ready to rule in favor of upholding automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: A propaganda war on the National Mall pits Trump against satirical statues and posters
item.description: The Trump administration and its critics are waging a war of images on the National Mall like none before. The president's face stares down from federal buildings while statues and posters mock him below.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration and its critics are waging a war of images on the National Mall like none before. The president's face stares down from federal buildings while statues and posters mock him below.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt
